steps.prompt: Which word is a relative pronoun?

steps.reveal: qui = who (nom. masc. pl., subject of 'audiunt')

hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'quibus' — 'qui' is nominative plural
hints.dative: Dative plural would be 'quibus' — 'qui' is nominative
hints.accusative: Accusative plural would be 'quos' — 'qui' is nominative

hints.to dare: That would be 'audere' — 'audiunt' comes from 'audire' (to hear)
hints.to increase: That would be 'augere' — 'audiunt' comes from 'audire' (to hear)
hints.to help: That would be 'adiuvare' — 'audiunt' comes from 'audire' (to hear)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'audiant' — 'audiunt' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'audierunt/audiverunt' — 'audiunt' is present indicative
hints.imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'audiebant' — 'audiunt' is present indicative

hints.audeo, audere, ausus sum, —: These are the parts of 'audere' (to dare)
hints.haurio, haurire, hausi, haustum: These are the parts of 'haurire' (to draw/drain)
hints.sentio, sentire, sensi, sensum: These are the parts of 'sentire' (to feel/perceive)

steps.reveal: verbum = the word (acc., direct object of 'audiunt')

hints.nominative: While 'verbum' looks identical in nominative, here it is object of 'audiunt'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'verbi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'verbi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'verbi' ends in -i

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Deus' — 'Dei' is genitive singular
hints.dative: Dative would be 'Deo' — 'Dei' is genitive, modifying 'verbum'

hints.genitive: Genitive singular would be 'beati', but with plural 'qui audiunt' it is nom. pl.
hints.dative: Dative would be 'beatis' — 'Beati' is nominative plural
hints.vocative: Vocative plural could be 'beati', but this is a declaration, not address

translation: Blessed are those who hear the word of God
